name: Lake Powell
description:
Lake Powell is the second largest man-made reservoir in the United States, stretching 186 miles through the spectacular red rock canyon country of southern Utah and northern Arizona. The lake's 1,960 miles of shoreline exceed the entire Pacific Coast, with countless side canyons, coves, and alcoves creating a maze of fishing opportunities. Striped bass are the marquee species, with schools of stripers chasing shad into the canyons and creating explosive surface-feeding frenzies that anglers can locate by watching for diving birds.

Multiple marinas including Wahweap, Bullfrog, and Halls Crossing provide full-service boat ramps, houseboat rentals, and supplies for fishing trips. Largemouth and smallmouth bass inhabit the rocky shorelines and submerged structure, with the warm canyon walls creating ideal bass habitat. Walleye have been stocked and are gaining a foothold in the deeper sections of the reservoir. Lake Powell's stunning scenery, including the iconic Rainbow Bridge and Navajo Mountain, makes every fishing trip a visual adventure through one of the most dramatic landscapes in the American West.

name: Flaming Gorge Reservoir
description:
Flaming Gorge Reservoir straddles the Utah-Wyoming border, with the Utah portion featuring the dramatic flaming red canyon walls that give the reservoir its name. The lake is one of the premier trophy lake trout fisheries in the West, with mackinaw exceeding 30 pounds caught regularly in the deep, cold waters of the canyon section. Kokanee salmon provide exciting light-tackle action and are a key forage species that fuels the growth of the lake's trophy predators.

The Utah side of Flaming Gorge offers excellent boat launch facilities at several marinas, including the popular Cedar Springs Marina near Dutch John. The Green River tailwater below Flaming Gorge Dam is world-renowned for its trophy brown and rainbow trout fishing, creating a unique two-for-one destination. Smallmouth bass have expanded throughout the reservoir and provide outstanding warm-water fishing along the rocky shorelines and points. The Flaming Gorge National Recreation Area surrounding the lake offers camping, hiking, and wildlife viewing in a spectacular high-desert canyon setting.

name: Strawberry Reservoir
description:
Strawberry Reservoir is Utah's premier trout fishery, a 17,164-acre mountain reservoir sitting at 7,600 feet elevation in the Wasatch Mountains east of Heber City. The reservoir produces rainbow trout and cutthroat trout of exceptional size and quality, with fish averaging 16 to 20 inches and trophy specimens exceeding 10 pounds caught each season. The Utah Division of Wildlife Resources manages the fishery with special regulations designed to produce quality over quantity, and the results speak for themselves.

Strawberry Reservoir features several well-maintained boat ramps and a marina, along with campgrounds and day-use facilities operated by the U.S. Forest Service. Kokanee salmon add diversity to the fishery and provide both a forage base for large trout and a fun light-tackle target during their fall spawning run. Shore fishing is productive along the dam face and rocky points, while boat anglers find success trolling and jigging over the deeper basins. The reservoir's high elevation means a shorter ice-free season, but ice fishing is extremely popular and productive when the lake freezes over each winter.

name: Deer Creek Reservoir
description:
Deer Creek Reservoir is a 2,965-acre impoundment in the scenic Heber Valley, positioned along the Provo River corridor between Heber City and Provo Canyon. The reservoir has developed into one of Utah's top walleye fisheries, with the Utah Division of Wildlife Resources managing the population to produce quality catches of fish in the 18- to 24-inch range. Largemouth bass provide warm-water action in the shallower bays and around the abundant docks and riprap along the shoreline.

Deer Creek State Park provides a well-maintained boat ramp and day-use facilities at this popular Wasatch Front fishing destination. Rainbow and brown trout inhabit the deeper, cooler sections of the reservoir and are particularly active in spring and fall. Yellow perch are abundant and offer excellent panfishing opportunities, especially during the winter ice fishing season. The reservoir's convenient location just 45 minutes from Salt Lake City and its beautiful mountain setting in the Heber Valley make it one of the most frequently visited fishing lakes in Utah.

name: Jordanelle Reservoir
description:
Jordanelle Reservoir is a 3,200-acre reservoir located between Park City and Heber City, offering outstanding bass fishing in a stunning mountain setting. The reservoir has become one of Utah's best largemouth and smallmouth bass fisheries, with both species thriving in the rocky structure and submerged terrain that characterizes the lake's shoreline. The bass regulations at Jordanelle have been tailored to promote trophy-class fish, and largemouth exceeding 5 pounds are caught with increasing frequency.

Jordanelle State Park operates boat ramps and recreation facilities on both the east and west sides of the reservoir. Rainbow and brown trout provide cold-water fishing opportunities in the deeper sections, and perch offer consistent panfishing action. The reservoir's location near the world-class ski resorts of Park City makes it a natural summer and fall destination for visitors already drawn to the area. Water levels can fluctuate based on irrigation and municipal water demands, but even at lower pool levels Jordanelle provides excellent fishing within easy reach of the Wasatch Front population centers.

name: Bear Lake
description:
Bear Lake is a stunning 112,000-acre natural lake straddling the Utah-Idaho border, known as the Caribbean of the Rockies for its remarkable turquoise-blue water color caused by suspended limestone particles. The lake is home to several endemic fish species found nowhere else in the world, including the Bear Lake whitefish, Bonneville cisco, and Bear Lake sculpin. Bonneville cutthroat trout, Utah's state fish, thrive in the cold, deep waters alongside lake trout that can exceed 30 pounds.

Bear Lake State Park on the Utah side provides boat ramps and beach access along the lake's western shore. The annual cisco run in January draws hundreds of anglers who dip-net these small endemic fish through the ice, a unique tradition dating back to the early settlement of the valley. Lake trout fishing is best during spring and fall when mackinaw move into shallower water, and rainbow trout provide consistent action throughout the open-water season. The lake's remote location in the Bear River Range of northern Utah ensures clean air, clear skies, and a peaceful fishing experience in one of the most visually striking lakes in the American West.
